topic.name: Imperial Japanese Navy Vessels
topic.description: The documents discuss various ships of the Imperial Japanese Navy, focusing on their construction, roles in battles, and historical significance during World War II and earlier conflicts.

Document 1: Zuihō class was a group of two aircraft carriers built for the Imperial Japanese Navy before World War II
Zuihō participated in the Philippine Sea and Leyte Gulf in mid-1944.
Document 2: Zuihō was a light aircraft carrier of the Imperial Japanese Navy
The ship participated in the Guadalcanal Campaign during the rest of 1942.
Document 3: Mikasa is a pre-dreadnought battleship built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the late 1890s
The ship served as the flagship of Admiral Tōgō Heihachirō throughout the Russo-Japanese War.

topic.name: State Highways in the US
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways in the United States, detailing their routes, histories, and connections to other roads.

Document 1: M-58 is a 5.108-mile-long east – west state trunkline highway in the US state of Michigan
The trunkline follows State Street through the Saginaw area
Document 2: Delaware Route 44 ( DE 44 ) is a state highway in Kent County , Delaware
The entire length of DE 44 is part of the National Highway System
Document 3: M-87 is the designation of a former state trunkline highway in the Lower Peninsula of the US state of Michigan
The highway served as a connector between US Highway 23 and the former routing of US 10
Document 4: Business M-28 was a state trunkline highway in the Upper Peninsula of Michigan
The business loop followed a U-shaped routing to connect downtown Newberry with M-28 south of town
Document 6: M-117 is a 14.479-mile state trunkline highway in the eastern Upper Peninsula of the US state of Michigan
The highway connects US Highway 2 with M-28 west of Newberry
Document 7: Old Baltimore Pike is a road in the U.S. state of Delaware
The road runs from Maryland Route 281 at the Maryland state line south of Newark, Delaware

topic.name: Music and Cultural Impact
topic.description: The documents discuss various songs and albums, highlighting their musical significance and cultural influence across different genres and eras.

Document 1: 'My Sweet Lord' topped charts worldwide and was the biggest-selling single of 1971 in the UK.
Harrison wrote 'My Sweet Lord' in praise of the Hindu god Krishna, blending religious elements.
Document 2: For Those Who Have Heart is the second studio album by American rock band A Day to Remember.
The album was generally well received by music critics.
Document 3: 'She Ain't You' is a song by American recording artist Chris Brown, reaching number five on the US Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs chart.
The song's lyrics are about Brown falling in and out of love.
Document 4: The Grammy Award for Best Concept Music Video was presented to recording artists for quality, concept music videos.
The Academy honors artistic achievement in the recording industry.
Document 5: 'Through the Rain' was meant to be an insight into Carey personal struggles throughout 2001.
The song has been well-received, with many complimenting its inspirational lyrical content.

Document 7: 'Run the World (Girls)' promotes female empowerment through its aggressive message and unique sound.
The song initially divided critics, showcasing its cultural impact.

topic.description: The documents discuss various professional wrestling events, tag teams, and notable matches within World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE).

topic.name: Climate Change Mitigation
topic.description: Strategies and actions aimed at reducing the impact of climate change through various means.

topic.name: Hurricane Seasons Overview
topic.description: The documents provide detailed accounts of various Pacific and Atlantic hurricane seasons, highlighting their impacts, meteorological histories, and significant storms.

Document 1: 1997 Pacific hurricane season was a very active hurricane season.
This was due to the exceptionally strong 1997 – 98 El Niño event.
Document 2: Hurricane Isidore was the ninth named storm and the second hurricane in the 2002 Atlantic hurricane season.
Its primary impact was the heavy rainfall which fell across southeast Mexico and from the central United States Gulf coast into the Ohio Valley.
Document 3: The 1965 Atlantic hurricane season was the first to use the modern-day bounds for an Atlantic hurricane season.
Hurricane Betsy was the strongest and most devastating storm of the season.
Document 4: Hurricane Octave was a Category 4 hurricane which existed during the above-average 1989 Pacific hurricane season.
The remnants of Atlantic Tropical Depression Nine tracked westward across the Caribbean Sea.
Document 5: The 1941 Texas hurricane was a large and intense tropical cyclone that struck coastal Texas as a major hurricane.
Damage from the storm amounted to about $ 6.5 million.
Document 6: The 1986 Pacific typhoon season has no official bounds; it ran year-round in 1986.
A total of 32 tropical depressions formed in 1986 in the Western Pacific.
Document 7: Hurricane Anna impacted Central America and the Windward Islands in July 1961.
Initially a tropical storm, it moved westward across the Caribbean Sea.

topic.name: Aquatic Species Overview
topic.description: This cluster discusses various aquatic species, including sharks and fish, along with some fungi found in aquatic environments.

Document 1: The longfin mako shark (Isurus paucus) is a species of mackerel shark in the family Lamnidae, with a probable worldwide distribution in temperate and tropical waters.
Longfin mako sharks are predators that feed on small schooling bony fishes and cephalopods.
Document 2: Toxotes chatareus, sometimes known by the common names seven-spot archerfish or largescale archerfish, is a species of perciform fish in the archerfish genus Toxotes.
They are omnivorous, feeding on insects, fish, and vegetative matter at the surface of the water.
Document 3: The epaulette shark (Hemiscyllium ocellatum) is a species of longtailed carpet shark, family Hemiscylliidae, found in shallow, tropical waters off Australia and New Guinea.
This shark feeds on a wide range of small benthic invertebrates and bony fishes.
Document 4: Limnoperdon is a fungal genus in the monotypic family Limnoperdaceae.
The species, described as new to science in 1976, produces fruit bodies that lack specialized structures such as a stem, cap and gills common in mushrooms.
Document 5: Agaricus hondensis, commonly known as the felt-ringed agaricus, is a species of fungus in the family Agaricaceae.
The mushroom is poisonous, and causes severe gastrointestinal upset if consumed.
Document 6: Hapalopilus nidulans (also known as Hapalopilus rutilans) is a species of polypore fungus in the family Polyporaceae.
This widely distributed species is found on five continents.
Document 7: The great white shark (Carcharodon carcharias) is a species of large lamniform shark which can be found in the coastal surface waters of all the major oceans.
The great white shark is mainly known for its size, with mature female individuals growing up to 6.1 m (20 ft) in length.

topic.name: Historical Castles in England and Scotland
topic.description: This topic encompasses the history, architecture, and significance of various medieval and early modern castles located in England and Scotland.

Document 1: Stokesay Castle is a fortified manor house in Stokesay, Shropshire, England.
It was built in the late 13th century by Laurence of Ludlow.
Document 2: Dirleton Castle is a medieval fortress in the village of Dirleton, East Lothian, Scotland.
The oldest parts of the castle date to the 13th century.
Document 3: Raglan Castle is a late medieval castle located just north of the village of Raglan in Monmouthshire, Wales.
The modern castle dates from between the 15th and early 17th centuries.
Document 4: St Margaret's Church is an Anglican church in the Ifield neighbourhood of Crawley, a town in West Sussex, England.
The present building incorporates the chancel from a 13th-century church.
Document 5: St Mawes Castle is an artillery fort constructed by Henry VIII near Falmouth, Cornwall, between 1540 and 1542.
During the English Civil War, St Mawes was held by Royalist supporters of King Charles I.
Document 6: Beeston Castle is a former Royal castle in Beeston, Cheshire, England, perched on a rocky sandstone crag.
It was built in the 1220s by Ranulf de Blondeville.
Document 7: Buckton Castle is a medieval ringwork near Carrbrook, Stalybridge, England.
It is listed as a Scheduled Ancient Monument due to its proximity to the Buckton Vale Quarry.

The game involves guiding a transparent ball containing one of four monkeys across maze-like platforms.
Document 2: Ratchet & Clank: Up Your Arsenal is a 2004 3D platform video game developed by Insomniac Games.
It follows Ratchet and Clank's adventure through a fictional universe to defeat a robotic villain.
Document 3: Halo is a military science fiction first-person shooter video game franchise created by Bungie.
The series centers on an interstellar war between humanity and a theocratic alliance of aliens known as the Covenant.
Document 4: Blood Money is a side-scrolling shooter video game developed by DMA Design.
The game is set in four different locations on a planet, where players are tasked with attacking the enemies.
Document 5: The development of L.A. Noire began in 2004 following the founding of its developer Team Bondi.
The game is notable for being the first to use the newly developed MotionScan technology.
Document 6: Dragon Quest is the first role-playing video game in the Dragon Quest media franchise.
Players control a hero character who is charged with saving the Kingdom of Alefgard.
Document 7: The Guardian Legend is a hybrid action-adventure/shoot 'em up video game developed by Compile.
The player controls a lone protagonist on a quest to destroy a large alien-infested world.

topic.name: Railway Transportation History
topic.description: The documents discuss various historical railway stations, their operations, and related infrastructure developments.

Document 1: The North Bank Depot Buildings , located in central Portland , Oregon , United States , are a pair of buildings formerly used as a freight warehouse and passenger terminal for the Spokane , Portland and Seattle Railway.
The buildings were listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1996.
Document 2: Grand Central Station was a passenger railroad terminal in downtown Chicago , Illinois , from 1890 to 1969.
The station opened December 8 , 1890 , closed November 8 , 1969 , and was demolished in 1971.
Document 4: Railroad Gazette was a trade journal first published in April 1856 that focused on railroad , transportation and engineering topics.
Master mechanics read and used the publication to share information about railway matters with one-another.
Document 6: The Lillesand – Flaksvand Line ( Norwegian : Lillesand – Flaksvandbanen ) or LFB was a 16.59-kilometer ( 10.31 mi ) railway between Flaksvand ( now called Flaksvatn ) and Lillesand in Aust-Agder , Norway.
The line opened on 4 June 1896 , and remained in use until 15 June 1953.
Document 7: Shore Line East ( SLE ) is a commuter rail service which operates along the Northeast Corridor through southern Connecticut , US.
The service was introduced in 1990 as a temporary measure to reduce congestion during construction work on I-95.

Document 3: The 2011 Daytona 500, the 53rd running of the event, was held on February 20, 2011 at Daytona International Speedway in Daytona Beach, Florida as the first race of the 2011 NASCAR Sprint Cup season.

Document 4: The Jaguar XJ220 is a two-seater supercar produced by British luxury car manufacturer Jaguar from 1992 until 1994.
The XJ220 made use of engineering work undertaken for Jaguar's then current racing car family.
Document 5: The 2015 Spanish Grand Prix, formally titled the Formula 1 Gran Premio de España Pirelli 2015, was a Formula One motor race held on 10 May 2015 at the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya in Montmeló, Spain.
Mercedes driver Nico Rosberg took his first win of the season, his first in Spain and the ninth of his career.
Document 7: The 2015 Singapore Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 20 September 2015 at the Marina Bay Street Circuit in Marina Bay, Singapore.
Defending race winner Lewis Hamilton of Mercedes entered as the leader in the World Drivers' Championship with 252 points.

topic.name: Popular American Dishes
topic.description: This topic encompasses a variety of iconic and culturally significant dishes commonly enjoyed in American cuisine.

Document 1: Crab dip, sometimes referred to as Maryland crab dip, is a thick, creamy dip that is typically prepared from cream cheese and lump crab meat.
It is sometimes served as an appetizer.
Document 2: A Bacon Explosion is a pork dish that consists of bacon wrapped around a filling of spiced sausage and crumbled bacon.
The Bacon Explosion became widely known after being posted on the BBQ Addicts blog.
Document 3: Fried chicken is a dish consisting of chicken pieces usually from broiler chickens which have been floured or battered and then pan-fried, deep fried, or pressure fried.
Fried chicken was first brought to the United States by Scottish immigrants.
Document 7: KFC (Kentucky Fried Chicken) was founded by Colonel Harland Sanders, an entrepreneur who began selling fried chicken from his roadside restaurant.
KFC popularized chicken in the fast-food industry, diversifying the market by challenging the established dominance of the hamburger.

topic.name: Mathematics and Physics Concepts
topic.description: This cluster discusses various mathematical and physical theories and principles, including Hilbert spaces, derivatives, Taylor series, and concepts in particle physics and electrical engineering.

Document 1: The mathematical concept of a Hilbert space, named after David Hilbert, generalizes the notion of Euclidean space.
Hilbert spaces arise naturally and frequently in mathematics and physics.
Document 2: The weak interaction is one of the four known fundamental interactions of nature.
The theory of the weak interaction is sometimes called quantum flavordynamics (QFD).
Document 3: The derivative of a function of a real variable measures the sensitivity to change of a quantity.
Derivatives are a fundamental tool of calculus.
Document 4: A Taylor series is a representation of a function as an infinite sum of terms that are calculated from the values of the function's derivatives.
A function can be approximated by using a finite number of terms of its Taylor series.
Document 5: Foster's reactance theorem is an important theorem in the fields of electrical network analysis and synthesis.
The theorem states that the reactance of a passive, lossless two-terminal network always strictly monotonically increases with frequency.
Document 6: Negative resistance is a property of some electrical circuits and devices in which an increase in voltage across the device's terminals results in a decrease in electric current through it.
Negative resistance can increase the power of an electrical signal, amplifying it.
Document 7: The roots of the reconstructed Proto-Indo-European language (PIE) are basic parts of words that carry a lexical meaning.
Complete inflected words like verbs, nouns or adjectives are formed by adding further morphemes to a root.
